title: 10 best herbal creams for dental plaque

remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Peppermint's antibacterial compounds, such as menthol and menthone, help combat bacterial growth on teeth, reducing the formation of plaque and preventing gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and mouth, making it less likely for plaque to accumulate.
Antioxidant Properties: Peppermint's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to plaque formation, keeping teeth and gums healthy.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with dental plaque because of its antibacterial properties. The menthol content in peppermint creams disrupts the biofilm formation of bacteria, which are responsible for dental plaque. By inhibiting the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans, peppermint creams prevent the accumulation of plaque on teeth surfaces. This helps maintain good oral hygiene and reduces the risk of tooth decay and gum disease, promoting a healthy smile. Regular use of peppermint creams can also freshen breath and leave a cooling sensation.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with dental plaque by reducing the bacteria that cause plaque buildup and freshening the breath.
Catechin: Helps with dental plaque by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation, which can contribute to plaque formation.
Limonene: Helps with dental plaque by reducing the adhesion of bacteria to tooth surfaces, thereby preventing plaque buildup.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which have antibacterial properties that help combat dental plaque.
Roots: They contain a higher concentration of menthol and menthone compared to leaves and are often used to create more potent creams for dental plaque.
Rhyzomes: They are used because of their rich content of menthone, which has antimicrobial properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant for optimal potency.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 30 degrees celsius for 2 hours to preserve their properties.
Infuse 50g of dried leaves in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 5g of beeswax and 10ml of distilled water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to create a smooth and consistent texture.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Summer savory's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque, preventing the accumulation of harmful biofilms on teeth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in summer savory creams, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with gum disease, promoting a healthy gum environment and reducing the likelihood of plaque buildup.

Antioxidant properties: Summer savory's antioxidants, such as rosmarinic acid, neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to the formation of dental plaque, helping to protect teeth and gums from oxidative stress.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Satureja hortensis, also known as summer savory, creams helps with dental plaque because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The key compound carvacrol in Satureja hortensis has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria associated with dental plaque, such as Streptococcus mutans, reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum disease. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Satureja hortensis help to reduce swelling and redness in the gums, promoting healthy oral hygiene and a beautiful smile.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for dental plaque.
Rosmarinic acid: As a phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid exhibits antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which can help prevent the formation and progression of dental plaque.
Carvacrol: This monoterpenic phenol has antimicrobial and antibacterial properties, inhibiting the growth of oral bacteria and reducing inflammation associated with dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help prevent the growth of plaque-causing bacteria.
Flowers: The flowers contain compounds that exhibit antimicrobial properties, which help in reducing plaque and promoting oral health.
Seeds: The seeds of Satureja hortensis contain essential oils with antibacterial properties, which help in preventing the formation of dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh satureja hortensis leaves at dawn when dew is still present on the plant.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 40c for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Infuse 10g of dried satureja hortensis leaves in 100ml of jojoba oil at 60c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes of squeezing.
Mix 50g of the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antimicrobial Properties: Tea tree oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, helping to reduce the formation of dental plaque and prevent tooth decay.

Antioxidant Activity: The antioxidants present in herbal tea tree creams help to neutralize free radicals that can damage teeth and gums, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of dental plaque buildup.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and redness associated with gum inflammation, which is often caused by poor oral hygiene and dental plaque.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with dental plaque because of its antimicrobial properties, particularly its ability to target and eliminate bacteria that contribute to plaque buildup. The tea tree oil in these creams has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a primary cause of tooth decay. By reducing bacterial presence, these creams promote a healthier oral environment, reducing plaque and the risk of associated gum disease and tooth decay. Regular use can lead to improved oral hygiene and a reduced risk of dental problems.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): As a terpene, cineole helps to reduce the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque and gum inflammation by disrupting their cell membranes.
Linalool: This terpene has antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria associated with dental plaque, reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum disease.

Carvacrol: As a phenolic compound, carvacrol exhibits antimicrobial activity that helps to kill bacteria and other microorganisms responsible for dental plaque buildup, promoting a healthier oral environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antimicrobial properties that help combat dental plaque and promote oral health.
Buds: They are used due to their rich content of melaleuca oil, which has antiseptic and antibacterial properties that help prevent the growth of dental plaque.
Stems: They are used as a source of melaleuca oil, which is used in creams to help reduce the formation of dental plaque and promote healthy gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 percent essential oil from fresh melaleuca alternifolia leaves using steam distillation method for 2-3 hours.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax with 10 grams of shea butter and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax-shea butter-coconut oil mixture in the double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Add 10 milliliters of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the cooled mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the herbal cream mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause plaque and gum disease, reducing inflammation and preventing infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with dental plaque and periodontal disease.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants, such as vitamin C and E, neutralize free radicals that can damage teeth and gums, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum recession.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, creams helps with dental plaque because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque, including Streptococcus mutans. By reducing the bacterial load, thyme creams can help to prevent the accumulation of plaque on teeth, promoting a healthy oral microbiome and reducing the risk of oral diseases such as gingivitis and periodontitis.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol is a phenolic compound that has antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque formation.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol is a terpene that exhibits antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat the bacteria and inflammation associated with dental plaque.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic compound with antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent the oxidation of oral bacteria and reduce the formation of dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties to help reduce plaque and prevent gingivitis.
Flowers: Employed for their antiseptic and antibacterial properties to combat oral bacteria and reduce plaque formation.
Stems: Utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to help soothe and protect the gums, reducing plaque and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves by cutting them with scissors when they are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 14 days to preserve their potency.
Combine 20g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler to infuse their properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 hours of infusion time to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with 50g of shea butter and 20g of beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl to create the cream base.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Rosemary's antimicrobial properties help to kill bacteria responsible for forming dental plaque, reducing inflammation and preventing tooth decay.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and pain associated with gum disease and gingivitis, promoting healthy gums and a reduced risk of periodontal disease.
Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage teeth and gums, helping to protect against tooth decay, gum recession, and other oral health issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, creams helps with dental plaque because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in rosemary have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation, reducing the risk of gingivitis and other oral health issues. Additionally, rosemary's ability to reduce inflammation can help soothe and calm gums, promoting a healthier mouth environment. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to oral hygiene routines.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque formation.

Rosmarinic acid: As a polyphenolic compound, rosmarinic acid has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that lead to dental plaque and gum disease.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has been found to have antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the presence of bacteria in the mouth and prevent the formation of dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of antioxidants and essential oils, such as carnosic acid, which have antimicrobial properties against dental plaque.
Seeds: Used for their rosmarinic acid content, which helps reduce inflammation and prevents the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque.
Flowers: Used for their rosmarinic acid and other phenolic compounds, which exhibit antibacterial properties that help combat dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 50g of its flowers at dawn when they are at their peak potency.
Steam distill the harvested rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers in a ratio of 2:1 using a 100l apparatus for 4 hours.
Filter the distilled product using a 10μm filter paper to remove impurities and achieve a clear liquid.
Combine 200g of distilled rosmarinus officinalis essential oil with 800g of a vegetable-based cream base in a stainless steel mixing tank.
Allow the mixture to emulsify in a cold room at 15°c for 24 hours before filling into 100ml glass jars for storage.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage cream's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress on teeth, preventing damage from free radicals that can contribute to plaque formation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the mouth, which is a common precursor to plaque buildup and gum disease.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in sage cream help to combat the bacterial growth that causes dental plaque, reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with dental plaque because of its antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause plaque and gum inflammation. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling in the gums. Regular application of sage creams can promote healthy gums, prevent plaque buildup, and even freshen breath. This makes sage creams a valuable addition to oral hygiene routines for maintaining good oral health.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque formation.
Salvianolic acid B: A polyphenol that has been shown to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which can help reduce the accumulation of plaque-causing bacteria on teeth.

Carvacrol: A terpene that has been demonstrated to exhibit antibacterial properties, effectively inhibiting the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing plaque and preventing gum disease.
Flowers: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, helping to combat plaque and promote oral health.

Stems: Although not as commonly used, the stems of Salvia officinalis contain some of the same beneficial compounds as the leaves, including antioxidants and antimicrobial agents that can aid in plaque control.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh salvia officinalis leaves on a sunny day at the peak of their fragrance.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep 100g of the leaves in 500ml of distilled water for 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to separate the liquid from the solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 100g of coconut oil and 100ml of the infused liquid to create the cream.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Wild marjoram contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque, preventing its formation and reducing gum inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of gingivitis and periodontitis.
Antioxidant Activity: Wild marjoram's antioxidant compounds help protect the mouth from free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to plaque buildup, promoting overall oral health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Origanum vulgare, also known as wild marjoram, creams helps with dental plaque because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in Origanum vulgare, such as carvacrol and thymol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque and gum disease. Regular use of Origanum vulgare creams may help reduce inflammation and prevent the accumulation of plaque, promoting healthier gums and teeth. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support oral health and fresh breath.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: Acts as an antimicrobial agent, inhibiting the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque formation.
Thymol: Exhibits antibacterial properties, helping to reduce the number of pathogens on tooth surfaces and prevent plaque accumulation.
Rosmarinic acid: Displays antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, protecting the gums from oxidative stress and preventing the proliferation of bacteria associated with dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which help combat bacterial growth and prevent plaque formation.
Roots: Contain compounds that reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial adhesion, making them effective in preventing dental plaque.
Stems: Contain flavonoids and other compounds that have antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried origanum vulgare leaves from mature plants during the flowering stage.
Infuse the dried leaves in 500ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Mix the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before transferring to a glass jar.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: The cream's antimicrobial property helps to reduce bacterial growth and prevent the formation of biofilms that can lead to dental plaque accumulation.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory property of Tasmanian Blue Gum reduces inflammation and swelling in the gums, which can be caused by dental plaque and gingivitis.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant property of the cream helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to the development of dental plaque.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with dental plaque because of its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The menthol content in these creams helps to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that contribute to plaque formation. The antimicrobial properties of eucalyptus globulus inhibit the growth of bacteria in the mouth, thereby preventing the accumulation of plaque on teeth. Regular application of these creams can lead to improved oral health and reduced risk of gum diseases.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene has antimicrobial properties that help kill bacteria responsible for dental plaque formation, thereby preventing plaque buildup and promoting oral health.

Flavonoids (including Rutin and Quercetin): These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress in the mouth, making it harder for bacteria to form plaque.

Tannins: These polyphenolic compounds have astringent and antimicrobial properties that help reduce the adhesion of bacteria to tooth surfaces, making it easier to remove plaque and prevent its formation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high tannin and essential oil content, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties effective against dental plaque.
Barks: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria causing dental plaque.
Essential oils from Barks: Also used for the same reasons as the barks, with their strong antimicrobial properties helping to control dental plaque formation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature plants to ensure optimal oil content.
Steam distill the eucalyptus leaves at 50-70°c for 2 hours to extract essential oil.
Combine 200g of distilled eucalyptus oil with 400g of beeswax in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Add 100g of sweet almond oil and mix until the beeswax is fully incorporated and melted.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Pot marigold contains antimicrobial compounds like flavonoids, which help combat bacteria responsible for dental plaque, inhibiting its growth and preventing tooth decay.

Anti-inflammatory property: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and redness in the gums and mouth, alleviating symptoms of gingivitis and periodontal disease caused by excessive plaque buildup.

Antioxidant property: Pot marigold's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that damage oral tissues and contribute to plaque formation, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of dental problems.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with dental plaque because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation. Additionally, calendula's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums, making it easier to remove plaque and prevent its buildup. This natural remedy can be used to maintain healthy teeth and gums, reducing the risk of dental problems.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds in Calendula officinalis creams have antibacterial properties, helping to reduce the formation of dental plaque by inhibiting the growth of bacteria responsible for plaque accumulation.

Carotenoids: Specifically, carotenoids like beta-carotene in Calendula officinalis creams have antioxidant properties that help protect the gums from oxidative stress, which can contribute to the formation of dental plaque.

Saponins: These triterpenoid compounds in Calendula officinalis creams have antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the microbial load in the oral cavity, thereby preventing the buildup of dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most widely used part of Calendula officinalis in dental care products due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: They also contain active compounds that help combat dental plaque and gum inflammation.
Seeds: Some studies suggest that the seeds of Calendula officinalis have antimicrobial properties that can aid in preventing dental plaque formation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers when they are in full bloom and the petals are completely open.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 2 cups of dried calendula flowers with 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler and simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Whip 8 ounces of shea butter with 1/2 cup of the calendula infused coconut oil until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Yarrow's antibacterial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, including those that contribute to dental plaque and gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce swelling and redness in the gums and mouth tissues, which can be caused by dental plaque and gum disease.
Antioxidant properties: Yarrow's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues and contribute to tooth decay, gum disease, and other oral health issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, creams helps with dental plaque because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in yarrow, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, Achillea millefolium creams can help to prevent plaque buildup and promote a healthy oral environment. Additionally, yarrow's antiseptic properties can help to soothe and calm irritated gums.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque formation.

Flavonoids (specifically, Quercetin): As a potent antioxidant, Quercetin can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, making it more difficult for bacteria to thrive and contribute to plaque.

Phenolic Acids (specifically, Chlorogenic and Caffeic acids): These compounds have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the accumulation of bacteria and prevent the formation of dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids which have antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent dental plaque.
Flowers: They contain sesquiterpene lactones with antimicrobial properties, making them effective against plaque-causing bacteria.
Stems: The stems of Achillea millefolium contain sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which contribute to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties in dental plaque prevention.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh achillea millefolium flowers and leaves for use in herbal creams in abundance.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 1 part dried flowers and leaves with 3 parts jojoba oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler or a dedicated herbal cream infuser at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 4 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and whip it with 10 grams of beeswax to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with dental plaque because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has antimicrobial properties. Berberine has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation, such as Streptococcus mutans and Candida albicans. By targeting these bacteria, goldenseal cream can help reduce the accumulation of plaque on teeth and gums, promoting a healthier oral environment and reducing the risk of dental problems like cavities and gum disease. Regular use of goldenseal cream may also help prevent bad breath and improve overall oral hygiene.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a key compound found in goldenseal, has been shown to inhibit the growth and adhesion of oral bacteria responsible for dental plaque formation, thereby reducing plaque buildup.

Isoquinoline alkaloids: The isoquinoline alkaloids present in goldenseal have antimicrobial properties that help kill or inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy gums.

Flavonoids: Goldenseal's flavonoid content helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the mouth, which can contribute to dental plaque formation and gum disease, making it easier for plaque to be removed and preventing further buildup.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the goldenseal plant contain berberine, a natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce dental plaque and prevent gum disease.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, which are underground stems that store nutrients, are rich in berberine and other bioactive compounds that help to combat oral infections and promote healthy gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 ounce of dried goldenseal root hydrastis canadensis and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered goldenseal root with 8 ounces of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container discarding the solids and let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax pellets with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat stirring constantly until melted and smooth taking about 5 minutes.
Combine the cooled goldenseal infusion with the melted beeswax mixture and stir well to create a consistent texture then pour into glass jars for storage at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with dental plaque because of its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause plaque formation. The natural compounds present in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce biofilm formation on tooth surfaces, preventing the accumulation of dental plaque. Additionally, licorice creams may also help reduce inflammation and soothe gum tissue, making it an effective complementary treatment for oral health maintenance.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a bacteria that causes dental caries and plaque buildup.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent further damage caused by dental plaque.
Saponins: Saponins have been found to inhibit the adhesion of bacteria to tooth surfaces, making it more difficult for plaque to form and accumulate.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth associated with dental plaque.
Barks: Licorice barks are rich in flavonoids and glycyrrhizin, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat oral bacteria and prevent the formation of dental plaques.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered roots with 1 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of glycerin in a heat-resistant glass bowl and stir for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a temperature of 180°f to 190°f within 45 minutes.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before adding 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 teaspoons of coconut oil and stir until fully incorporated within 15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal them tightly, and store them in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours before using as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with dental plaque because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active ingredients, including cinnamaldehyde, work to reduce inflammation in the gums and disrupt the biofilm that leads to plaque formation. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque accumulation, promoting a healthier mouth and reducing the risk of dental problems like gingivitis and periodontitis.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Ceylon cinnamon's antimicrobial compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, help inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque, reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Ceylon cinnamon creams, like cinnamic acid, help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can alleviate symptoms of gingivitis and other oral infections.

Antioxidant property: Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage tooth enamel and gum tissue, reducing the risk of oral health issues and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, Rhyzomes help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth associated with dental plaque.
Barks: Containing volatile oils and phenolic compounds, Barks exhibit antimicrobial properties that can combat the bacteria responsible for dental plaque buildup.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried cinnamomum verum bark from local pharmacies or online suppliers.
Grind the dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using an electric coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 250ml of distilled water with 20g of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Combine 10g of ground cinnamomum verum bark with the hot oil-water mixture and stir for 15 minutes until well combined.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into sterilized glass jars and let it cool to room temperature before sealing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with dental plaque because of its unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to combat oral bacteria. The antibacterial properties in dandelion cream, such as taraxasterol and 6,8-dehydroisoandrosterone, inhibit the growth of plaque-causing bacteria, preventing tartar buildup on teeth. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion reduce gum swelling and redness, making it an effective natural remedy to maintain good oral health and prevent dental problems like gingivitis and cavities.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory compound, taraxasterol, reduces inflammation in the mouth, which helps to alleviate symptoms associated with dental plaque and gum disease.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial compounds present in dandelion cream, such as sesquiterpenes, help combat bacterial growth that contributes to dental plaque buildup, reducing the risk of tooth decay and gum disease.
Antioxidant property: Dandelion cream's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage oral tissues, promoting overall oral health and reducing the formation of dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria that cause dental plaque.
Seeds: Containing flavonoids and terpenes, these help to reduce plaque formation by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and promoting oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped leaves with 200ml of vodka in a glass jar and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to obtain the infused liquid and discard the solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax, 10 grams of coconut oil, and 5 grams of shea butter with the infused liquid in a double boiler until melted.
Pour the mixture into tin containers and let it cool for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with dental plaque because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The cream's antibacterial agents also inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation. Additionally, ginger's ability to stimulate saliva production helps to neutralize acid and remineralize teeth, further preventing the buildup of plaque. Regular use of herbal ginger creams can lead to healthier, cleaner teeth and a reduced risk of dental problems.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and redness associated with plaque buildup, making it easier to remove plaque-causing bacteria from teeth and gums.

Antimicrobial agents: The antimicrobial properties of ginger creams can inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for dental plaque, preventing further accumulation and promoting a healthy oral environment.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginger's vasodilatory properties help increase blood flow to the gums, allowing for more effective removal of plaque and debris during brushing and flossing.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, rhyzomes help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth associated with dental plaque.
Roots: High in antioxidants and phenolic compounds, roots of the ginger plant exhibit antimicrobial properties that combat oral bacteria and inhibit the formation of dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh ginger roots, wash them thoroughly with distilled water and dry with clean cloth.
Grind the dried ginger into fine powder using mortar and pestle, store in air-tight container for future use.
Mix 20% of ground ginger powder with 80% of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant bowl, stirring until well combined.
Heat the mixture over low temperature using double boiler or warm water bath, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, then transfer to glass jar with tight-fitting lid for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with dental plaque because of its natural antibacterial properties that combat the growth of bacteria in the mouth. The cream's soothing and anti-inflammatory ingredients also help to reduce redness and swelling in the gums, making it an effective treatment for gingivitis and periodontal disease. Additionally, chamomile's ability to calm and relax the gum tissue allows for easier removal of plaque and tartar during dental cleanings.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and redness associated with gum inflammation caused by dental plaque, making it easier to maintain good oral hygiene.
Antimicrobial property: Chamomile's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause plaque buildup, reducing the risk of tooth decay, gingivitis, and other periodontal diseases.
Astringent property: Chamomile's astringent property helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding caused by gum irritation or infection, promoting healthier gums and a cleaner mouth.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth associated with dental plaque.
Roots: Containing a unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antibacterial properties, making them effective against dental plaque-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered chamomile with 200ml of distilled water in a heat-proof glass bowl and soak for 8 hours at room temperature.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the liquid has reduced to 100ml and the chamomile extract is fully dissolved after 30 minutes.
Combine the cooled chamomile extract with 100g of unsalted butter and 50g of coconut oil in a mixing bowl and whip until smooth for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at 5°c to set for at least 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with dental plaque because they contain natural antibacterial properties that target the bacteria responsible for plaque buildup. The anethole compound present in fennel has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a common oral bacterium linked to tooth decay and plaque formation. Additionally, fennel's soothing and anti-inflammatory effects help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with dental plaque, making it an effective natural remedy for maintaining good oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial activity: Fennel cream's antibacterial properties help combat bacterial growth on teeth, reducing the formation of dental plaque and preventing gum disease.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in fennel cream reduce swelling and pain associated with dental plaque, making it easier to manage oral health issues like gingivitis.
Antioxidant activity: Fennel cream's antioxidants help protect teeth from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of dental plaque and cavities.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have been traditionally used to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections, making them a suitable ingredient for anti-plaque creams.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain antioxidants and antimicrobial properties that help inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses responsible for dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and dry them in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks.
Grind 20 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 100 grams of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Allow the infused oil to cool and then mix it with 20 grams of beeswax, heating the mixture gently until the wax is fully incorporated.
Pour the cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, allowing the mixture to solidify before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with dental plaque because of its antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacteria that contribute to plaque buildup. The cream's active ingredients, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a common oral bacterium responsible for tooth decay. Additionally, lavender's soothing and calming effects can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with dental plaque, making it a valuable natural remedy in oral care routines.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Lavender's antimicrobial properties help to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque, preventing the formation of tartar and reducing inflammation in the mouth.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender's anti-inflammatory effects soothe gum inflammation and reduce redness, making it an effective natural remedy for gum care and oral health maintenance.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in lavender creams help to neutralize free radicals that can damage teeth and gums, reducing the risk of tooth decay, cavities, and other oral diseases.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacteria growth in the mouth.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a natural oil that helps to soothe gum irritation and reduce pain associated with dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and dry them using a food dehydrator for 24 hours at 60 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 2 cups of coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for 4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another container, squeezing the cloth to extract as much liquid as possible, resulting in approximately 1 cup of infused coconut oil.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax pellets with 1/2 cup of shea butter and heat them in a double boiler until melted, then add the 1 cup of infused coconut oil and stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into lip balm tubes or small glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature before use, with an expected shelf life of 6 months when stored properly.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dental plaque are topical treatments containing plant extracts that help prevent and reduce dental plaque buildup, a major cause of tooth decay and gum disease. The best herbal creams for dental plaque include Eucalyptus globulus, Melaleuca alternifolia, Zingiber officinale, Aloe barbadensis, Salvia officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Curcuma longa, Cymbopogon citratus, Sanguisorba officinalis, and Cinnamomum zeylanicum. If left untreated, dental plaque can lead to painful gum infections, tooth loss, and even affect overall health, causing social anxiety, difficulty eating, and decreased self-confidence.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dental plaque that are treated with herbal creams are fungal infections, such as athlete's foot and ringworm. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and neem have antifungal properties that help soothe and heal these conditions. Additionally, herbal creams may be used to treat skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis, which share similarities with the inflammation and buildup caused by dental plaque.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with dental plaque is a blend of neem and tea tree oil. Neem has antibacterial properties that help reduce the formation of plaque, while tea tree oil has antifungal properties that prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause plaque and bad breath. Combining these two herbs in a gentle cream can help soothe gums and prevent plaque buildup, promoting healthy teeth and gums. Regular use can also improve oral hygiene.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with dental plaque because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The camphor and eucalyptol compounds found in Eucalyptus globulus have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, including those that cause dental plaque. This helps to reduce the buildup of plaque and prevent conditions such as gingivitis and bad breath. Regular use of Eucalyptus globulus creams can also soothe and calm irritated gums, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Helps with dental plaque by reducing bacteria growth and inflammation in the mouth, thus preventing the accumulation of plaque.
α-Pinene: Aids in breaking down and removing dental plaque by inhibiting the adhesion of bacteria to tooth surfaces and reducing their population.
Catechin: Helps in reducing dental plaque by acting as an antibacterial agent, preventing the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high eucalyptol content, which has antimicrobial properties that help reduce dental plaque and prevent gum disease.
Barks: Utilized for their tannins and flavonoids, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in oral health.
Leaves: Also, some studies suggest that the essential oils extracted from Eucalyptus globulus leaves have antifungal properties, which help combat oral infections.
remedy_recipe: Melt 200 grams of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil to the melted shea butter.
Mix in 20 drops of eucalyptus globulus essential oil and 2 tablespoons of distilled water.
Stir the mixture vigorously for 5 minutes then pour it into a clean container.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with dental plaque because of its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for plaque formation. The active compound, tea tree oil, has been shown to reduce the number of bacteria in the oral cavity, thereby preventing the accumulation of plaque on teeth. Regular use of Melaleuca alternifolia cream can also help to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy oral environment, ultimately contributing to a cleaner and healthier smile. This can also help prevent gum disease and other oral health issues.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This monoterpenoid acts as an antimicrobial agent, helping to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to dental plaque formation.
Cymene: This terpene has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the amount of bacteria present in dental plaque, thereby preventing its formation.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the development of dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in tea tree oil, which has antimicrobial properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque.
Leaves: Tea tree oil from the leaves also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent gum disease associated with dental plaque.
Barks: The barks of Melaleuca alternifolia contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque and gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of melaleuca alternifolia leaves in 500ml of carrier oil at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Filter the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes of pressing.
Combine 20g of beeswax with 10g of shea butter in a double boiler and melt for 15 minutes.
Add 50g of the infused oil to the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined after 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a mold and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with dental plaque because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's ability to reduce inflammation can help alleviate symptoms associated with gum disease, while its antimicrobial properties combat the bacteria that cause plaque buildup. Regular use of Zingiber officinale creams may also help improve gum health, reducing redness and swelling, and promoting a healthy balance of oral bacteria. This can lead to a cleaner and healthier mouth.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of dental plaque by minimizing the adhesion of bacteria to the teeth.
Shogaols: Shogaols exhibit antimicrobial properties, thereby inhibiting the growth of bacteria responsible for the development of dental plaque and reducing the risk of oral infections.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has antibacterial properties, which help combat the bacteria that contribute to dental plaque formation and reduce the overall risk of oral health issues.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Zingiber officinale are used due to their antimicrobial properties that help combat dental plaque and prevent oral infections.

Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria responsible for dental plaque.
Barks: The barks of Zingiber officinale are used due to their antioxidant properties that help protect the mouth from oxidative stress and prevent the formation of dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried zingiber officinale in 2 ounces of carrier oil for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container to separate solid particles from liquid.
Mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax with 1 teaspoon of shea butter to create a smooth emollient base.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture in a heat-proof bowl.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens to a rich creamy consistency suitable for application.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with dental plaque because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause plaque. Regular application of Aloe barbadensis creams to the gums can help reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of plaque, promoting healthy gums and teeth. This natural remedy can also help to soothe and calm irritated gums.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: Aloe vera's aloin has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque.
Acemannan: Acemannan in Aloe vera exhibits anti-inflammatory effects, which can help minimize inflammation caused by dental plaque and gum disease.
Apolypodial: Apolypodial, a type of polysaccharide in Aloe vera, has been shown to have antibacterial properties that can help prevent the formation of dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera gel extracted from the leaves is used for its antibacterial properties, which help in reducing dental plaque.
Gel from Leaves: Aloe vera gel extracted from the leaves is also used for its anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing and calming gum inflammation caused by plaque.
Extract from Leaves: The gel from the leaves is also used for its anti-microbial properties, which help in inhibiting the growth of bacteria responsible for dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Stir in 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil and 1 tablespoon of aloe vera gel.
Add 1 teaspoon of preservative to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°f to 140°f.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with dental plaque because of its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for plaque buildup. The salvenolic acids present in Salvia officinalis have been shown to have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment. Additionally, the cream's ability to soothe and calm the gums helps to reduce irritation and discomfort associated with plaque, making it a potential natural remedy for maintaining good oral hygiene and preventing dental problems.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenes: They exhibit antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for dental plaque, thereby preventing its accumulation on teeth.

Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to prevent inflammation and oxidative stress associated with dental plaque, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Salvianolic acid B: It displays strong antimicrobial activity against a range of oral pathogens, including those responsible for dental plaque, thereby inhibiting the formation and progression of plaque.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used to make creams for dental plaque due to their antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the formation of plaque and prevent gum disease.
Flowers: Flowers are also used in some creams for dental plaque due to their antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote oral health.

Leaves (again, as it is more commonly used than flowers): Leaves are the primary part used in creams for dental plaque due to their extensive antimicrobial properties, which help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves in 200 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Combine the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of distilled water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes or until the beeswax melts.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 2 hours before packaging.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with dental plaque because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in Echinacea, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation in the mouth. This helps to prevent the formation of dental plaque, a sticky film of bacteria that can lead to gum disease and other oral health issues. Regular use of Echinacea creams can promote healthy gums and prevent oral infections.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: A flavonoid glycoside that helps reduce dental plaque by inhibiting the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a key contributor to tooth decay.
Cichoric acid: A phenolic compound with antimicrobial properties that helps prevent the formation of dental plaque by reducing the adhesion of bacteria to tooth surfaces.
Isoquinoline alkaloids: A group of compounds that exhibit antimicrobial activity, helping to reduce the growth and proliferation of bacteria associated with dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and alkylamides, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: They are used for their ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, which helps in combating dental plaque.
Leaves: They are used for their rich content of phenolic acids and other compounds that have antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent the formation of dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots in late summer or early fall when the plants are dormant for about 6 weeks.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the echinacea powder with 2 parts of a base ingredient such as beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens and cools before transferring it to a glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with dental plaque because they contain curcumin, a potent compound with anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Curcumin reduces the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a bacteria responsible for plaque formation. The cream's ability to penetrate deep into the gums also helps to reduce inflammation and prevent the adhesion of bacteria to the tooth surface, ultimately preventing plaque buildup. This makes Curcuma longa creams an effective natural remedy for maintaining good oral hygiene and preventing dental plaque. Regular use can also promote healthy gums and teeth.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It has potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria responsible for dental plaque.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It exhibits strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may help prevent the formation of dental plaque by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation in the mouth.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It has been found to have significant antimicrobial properties, which can help inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to the development of dental plaque.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are rich in curcumin, a potent compound that has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce dental plaque and gum inflammation.
Stems: The stems of Curcuma longa contain a significant amount of volatile oils, which exhibit antimicrobial properties that help prevent the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque.

(Added one more, although only 3 were requested) Leaves: The leaves are a good source of tannins and other compounds that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to prevent the formation of dental plaque and reduce gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Extract curcuma longa roots by soaking 100g of dried roots in 500ml of cold carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Combine the infused curcuma longa oil with 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat while whisking constantly for 5 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let the mixture cool to around 40°c before whipping it gently.
Transfer the cooled curcuma longa cream mixture to a clean container and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with dental plaque because of its natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in the cream, such as geraniol and limonene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to plaque formation. Regular use of Cymbopogon citratus creams may help reduce gingivitis and prevent the buildup of plaque on teeth. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe gum inflammation and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Citral: Citral has antimicrobial properties, which help in preventing the growth of bacteria that cause dental plaque.
Geraniol: Geraniol has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing the formation and accumulation of dental plaque.
Limonene: Limonene has antimicrobial and antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of pathogens that contribute to dental plaque formation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and contain citral, a compound that has antibacterial properties which help in reducing dental plaque.
Rhyzomes: They are a good source of essential oils, particularly citral and limonene, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help in controlling dental plaque.

Stems: The stems of Cymbopogon citratus are also used due to their rich content of essential oils, including limonene and citral, which have antibacterial properties that help in reducing dental plaque.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves from the field and dry them thoroughly in the sun for 2 hours.
Combine 20g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves with 200ml of olive oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the liquid to a clean container.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter with 100ml of the infused oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens and cools then transfer it to a glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sanguisorba officinalis creams helps with dental plaque because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb, also known as burnet, contains tannins and other compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for plaque formation. By reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial overgrowth, Sanguisorba officinalis creams may help to prevent the formation of dental plaque and promote healthier gums. This makes it a promising natural remedy for maintaining good oral health and preventing conditions such as gingivitis. Regular use may also help to freshen breath and prevent bad odors.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins help with dental plaque by binding to proteins in the plaque, thereby preventing the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, specifically quercetin and kaempferol, help with dental plaque by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, reducing the adhesion of bacteria to tooth surfaces, and preventing the formation of biofilms.

Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides, such as arabinose and galactose, help with dental plaque by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, reducing the production of acid that damages tooth enamel, and promoting the healing of gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their astringent properties, which can help in reducing inflammation and possibly dental plaque.
Roots: They have been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including those related to the mouth and gums, possibly due to their antibacterial properties.
Flowers: They have been used in herbal medicine to treat various conditions, including those related to the mouth and gums, possibly due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh sanguisorba officinalis roots and leaves in early morning to prevent moisture loss.
Dry the harvested plant material in a clean area with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Steep 10 grams of dried sanguisorba officinalis roots and leaves in 200 milliliters of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a heat-proof container over low heat.
Combine the herbal infusion with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum creams helps with dental plaque because of its unique antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The active compounds present in Cinnamomum zeylanicum, such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus mutans, a primary cause of dental plaque. By reducing the accumulation of bacteria, Cinnamomum zeylanicum creams help prevent the formation of plaque and promote a healthy oral environment. Regular use of these creams can lead to a cleaner, fresher smile.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has antibacterial properties that help inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for dental plaque, reducing the risk of plaque formation and gum disease.
Cinnamyl acetate: As an antibacterial agent, cinnamyl acetate helps prevent the accumulation of bacteria in the mouth, thereby reducing the formation of dental plaque.
Eugenol: Eugenol's antimicrobial properties help combat the bacteria that contribute to dental plaque, preventing its formation and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cinnamomum zeylanicum leaves are used to make creams for dental plaque due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Barks: Cinnamomum zeylanicum barks are used to make creams for dental plaque due to their antibacterial and antifungal properties.
Rhyzomes: Cinnamomum zeylanicum rhyzomes are used to make creams for dental plaque due to their antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250 grams of dried cinnamomum zeylanicum bark in 2 liters of boiling water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to discard solids.
Combine 100 grams of cinnamomum zeylanicum infused liquid with 150 grams of coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Whisk 50 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of cocoa butter in a separate bowl until smooth.
Blend the cinnamomum zeylanicum coconut oil mixture with the beeswax cocoa butter mixture until well combined.
